filter_index.video_title: Bijli Mahadev Trek | Easy Trek near Manali | Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: January and February: possible snow on surrounding mountains (Pir Panjal range); trail itself usually does not have snow
March: snow may still be present but melts by April
Monsoon (July to September): trail can be slippery and somewhat challenging due to rain
Landslides and roadblocks more frequent during monsoon, can cause traffic jams and delays en route to base camp
Monsoon treks are usually safe and Indiahikes only runs monsoon treks that are safely navigable in the rains
temperature_notes: Monsoon (Jul–Sep): daytime 18–22°C, night 12–14°C; 2–3 warm layers (colder if it rains)

experience_suitability: Suitable for children / introducing children to trekking in the Himalayas
terrain_challenges: Mostly flat trail winding through rolling hills
Initial 10-15 minutes of ascent through Kot Village is steep
Trails can get slushy and uncomfortable to walk on during heavy rain
Initial 10-15 minutes of ascent through Kot Village is steep and can test mental preparedness
altitude_or_weather_risks: Can get sunny, especially during spring and summer
Heavy rain can make trails slushy and uncomfortable
Team monitors weather and may turn back if conditions deteriorate
Dehydration is common if fluids are insufficient while walking up the slope

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog, or run 5 km in under 60 minutes (recommended fitness target)
approval_requirements: No formal Fitness Approval Process required
Screenshots of walks/runs do not need to be submitted

experience_themes: Autumn: clean landscapes, clear skies, vibrant green forest, orange hues by November, villagers preparing for winter
Winter: peaceful and calm atmosphere, light snow dusting on surrounding Pir Panjal range, green deodar forests contrasting with snowy peaks, crisp air
Spring: lush green deodar forests, distant snow-capped Pir Panjal peaks, calm and peaceful trail
Summer: warm but pleasant, cool shaded deodar forest trail, vibrant village life
Monsoon: vibrant green paradise, mist and fog, mystical cloud-covered trails, lush greenery, full streams
Autumn: clean landscapes, clear skies, vibrant green forest turning orange by November
primary_watchouts: Monsoon: slippery and challenging trail due to rain
Monsoon: landslides and roadblocks en route to base camp causing delays
Monsoon: reach pickup town a day earlier and keep a buffer day after trek
Winter nights drop to 2–3°C requiring 4 warm layers
Mostly flat trail winding through rolling hills
best_for: Autumn: photography enthusiasts wanting clean landscapes and clear skies; those wanting to see mountains clearly
Winter: trekkers wanting winter trekking experience without harsh snow conditions
Spring: beginners wanting Himalayan beauty without extreme conditions
Summer: trekkers wanting to experience mountain village life
Monsoon: trekkers seeking lush greenery, misty trails, and a unique experience
